exemplar.title: Investigating if classical music with no lyrics increases a Grade 11 student's recall ability compared to rap music with lyrics of a list of 15 words to test the irrelevant sound effect.

detailedExemplarData.title: Investigating if classical music with no lyrics increases a Grade 11 student's recall ability compared to rap music with lyrics of a list of 15 words to test the irrelevant sound effect.

subject.title: Psychology
subject.description:
IB Psychology is a Group 3 subject in the International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP). The course examines human behavior and mental processes through scientific inquiry, exploring biological, cognitive, and sociocultural approaches to understanding behavior. Students develop critical thinking skills while investigating contemporary psychological research, research methods, and ethical considerations in psychological practice.

The course is available at Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours) and Higher Level (HL, 240 hours), with first assessment in 2027.

Assessment consists of Paper 1 (integration of concepts, content, and contexts through short-answer and extended-response questions: SL 2 hours, HL 2 hours, SL 50%, HL 40%), Paper 2 (application of psychological knowledge to research contexts: SL 1 hour, HL 2 hours, SL 25%, HL 20%), Paper 3 (HL only: research methods and data analysis, 1 hour, 20%), and Internal Assessment (research proposal development: SL 25%, HL 20%).

strengths: Clear, concise aim linking music conditions to student study habits and the phonological loop
Accurate description of Baddeley & Hitchâs workingâmemory model with explicit connection to the present investigation
Precisely stated directional and null hypotheses with operationalised IV and DV
weaknesses: Minor vagueness in framing the rationaleâno explicit reference to gaps in existing literature
Some textbookâlike phrasing in the theory description; link to investigation could be tightened
Literature summary (Perham & Sykora) is overly detailed and could be more focused
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student fulfils the 5â6 band descriptors for aim and relevance (clear aim with explained relevance), and for theory/model (described with explicit link). The hypothesis/variables strand is executed at the top level (band 6). Minor issues in literature contextualisation and wording prevent the absolute top mark, so a holistic score of 5 out of 6 reflects this strong yet slightly imperfect performance.

strengths: Independentâmeasures design is well explained with valid rationale for reducing practice effects and demand characteristics
Controlled variables and materials are systematically detailed with clear explanations of how they enhance validity
Participant characteristics (demographics, language proficiency) and random allocation are described, supporting internal and ecological validity
weaknesses: Sampling justification omits discussion of potential bias and effects on generalisability
Some procedural descriptions are incomplete (e.g., environmental control steps)
Materials selection (word list characteristics) could be better linked to pilot test outcomes
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Research design and control of variables/materials meet the âexplainedâ level (band 4). Sampling is adequately described but justification is brief (band 3). Overall, the studentâs exploration work demonstrates thorough understanding and explanation, meriting the top mark in this criterion (4 out of 4).

strengths: Appropriate use of mean, standard deviation, and a non-parametric Mann-Whitney U test with accurate reporting of p-value and decision
Results interpretation clearly links statistical findings to data and the hypothesis, rejecting the null with coherent reasoning
Tables effectively display descriptive statistics, facilitating comparison between conditions
weaknesses: Graphical presentation has errors: incomplete axis labels, y-axis scaling, and title inaccuracies
No explicit justification for choosing a non-parametric test and omission of effect size reporting
Table caption lacks clarity on which statistics correspond to each condition
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Statistical analysis is applied accurately with minor methodological justification missing (band 5). The graph addresses the hypothesis but contains presentation errors (band 3). Interpretation of findings is strong and linked to theory (band 5). On balance, the performance aligns with the 5â6 band descriptors, earning a 5 out of 6 for this criterion.

strengths: Modifications are explicitly linked to identified limitations and are well justified
Findings discussion references the working-memory model and previous research, reinforcing theoretical grounding
Strengths and limitations are relevant to the investigation and are explained in context
weaknesses: Limited exploration of alternative explanations (e.g., primacy/recency effects) in the discussion
Some repetition in the narrative; could be streamlined to emphasize novel insights
External validity issues are noted but not deeply explored
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All strands meet the âexplainedâ level of the top band (band 5), but slight unevenness in depth, some repetitive discussion, and missed opportunities for broader critical evaluation suggest a performance at the lower end of that band. To align with the overall mark constraint, a holistic score of 4 out of 6 is appropriate.

strandSelections.reasoning: A bar chart correctly contrasts mean recall for each condition, addressing the hypothesis.  However, axis labels are minimal, the y-axis is not fully scaled to 15, and the title repeats wording errors (âno lyricsâ twice).  These presentation flaws align with âgraph addresses the hypothesis but contains errors,â placing it in band 3-4; 3 points reflect moderate quality.

The results section clearly interprets statistical findings (significant at p < .01) in relation to data, rejects the null, and links outcome to phonological overload.  Interpretation is explicitly tied to both the quantitative results and the hypothesis, fulfilling band 5-6.  Slight redundancy and limited discussion of effect size prevent the maximum, so 5 is awarded.

Both IV and DV are precisely stated and operationalised: IV = presence/absence of lyrics (rap vs classical), DV = number of correctly recalled words from a 15-word list.  The hypotheses are written in directional and null form with the operational terms embedded.  No ambiguity is left about measurement, satisfying the highest requirements; hence the top score of 6 is justified.

Opportunity sampling is described and its practicality in the school context plus random allocation to conditions are explained.  The student also notes demographic features and ethical eligibility.  While justification is present, it is brief and does not fully consider alternative strategies or sample characteristicsâ impact on validity, so a mid-band mark of 3 is appropriate.

Baddeley & Hitchâs workingâmemory model is outlined with all main components named and briefly described, followed by an explicit explanation of how lyrics would overload the phonological loop and impair recall.  The connection between theory and present investigation is therefore clearly explained rather than merely mentioned.  Some wording is textbookâlike and the link could be tightened (e.g., no mention of articulatory suppression), so a solid but not perfect performance merits 5 within the top band.

The student integrates findings with the working memory model and with Perham & Sykoraâs study, explaining how the data corroborate theoretical claims about the phonological loop.  While coherent and theory-anchored, the discussion is concise and could further explore alternative explanations; thus, 5 within the top band is reasonable.

The aim â to compare recall under classical (noâlyrics) versus rap (lyrics) music â is stated in a clear, single sentence and immediately followed by a justification linked to studentsâ study habits and the phonological loop.  The student explicitly explains why the answer would be âcompellingâ and how results could be âutilised to inform students.â  This moves well beyond a mere identification of relevance, fulfilling the descriptor for band 5-6.  Minor vagueness (e.g., no reference to existing gaps in literature) prevents awarding the top of the band.

Multiple strengths (control of timing, independent measures) and several specific limitations (participant variability, contamination, ecological validity) are each followed by a brief explanation of their impact on validity.  Coverage is relevant and sufficiently detailed to reach the âexplainedâ level; slight uneven depth justifies 5 rather than 6.

Controlled variables (time of day, display timing, environment), materials (pre-recorded videos, word list, consent forms) and their rationales are systematically detailed, with explicit statements of how each control enhances validity.  This clear explanation fulfils the âexplainedâ criterion at the top of band 3-4, justifying 4.

For every stated limitation, the student proposes a directly linked, feasible modification (matched-pairs, random sampling, simultaneous testing, concept-based task) and justifies how each would resolve the issue.  Explanations are clear though not extensively elaborated, meriting 5 in the highest band.

The student not only describes the independent-measures design but explains why it reduces practice effects and demand characteristics, and how it improves internal validity.  That explicit rationale meets the âexplainedâ level of band 3-4, and coverage is thorough enough to justify the upper mark of 4.

Mean, SD and an appropriate non-parametric Mann-Whitney U test for independent groups with small sample and non-normal assumptions are applied.  The student states critical values, p-value, and decision about hypotheses accurately.  Minor methodological justification (why non-parametric) is missing, therefore 5 rather than 6.

annotations.comment: The opening description of memory is clear but too general; it would benefit from specifying a research gap or situating the investigation within recent empirical studies to strengthen the rationale.
The working memory model is succinctly described with accurate component definitions, demonstrating strong theoretical understanding and a clear link to cognitive processes.
While the Perham and Sykora study is relevant, the summary is overly detailed; consider focusing on core findings and methodological parallels to streamline the literature review.
The connection between theory and the current study is stated, but you could strengthen this by explicitly noting how this investigation fills a gap or extends the existing research.
The aim is clearly stated and justified with relevance to student study habits and the phonological loop, demonstrating strong alignment with band 5â6 descriptors.
The dependent variable is precisely defined and operationalised, eliminating ambiguity in measurement of recall performance.
Hypotheses are clearly stated in directional and null form with correct identification of IV and DV, reflecting top-level operationalisation.
The independent-measures design is well explained with valid rationale for reducing practice effects and demand characteristics, enhancing internal validity.
Opportunity sampling is justified by practicality, but consider discussing potential sampling bias and how it might affect generalisability to strengthen the rationale.
Participant characteristics are described in detail, including language proficiency and demographic balance, which supports internal and ecological validity.
Controlled variables are detailed, but the description cuts off mid-sentence; ensure completeness and clarify the environmental control procedure.
The noise-isolated classroom control enhances internal validity by minimizing extraneous auditory interference.
Materials are systematically chosen and piloted, but you could justify the selection of 2â3 syllable abstract words by linking this directly to pilot test results.
The procedural steps are clearly listed, but step 1 (âmoved outsideâ) is unclear in context; clarify classroom logistics to avoid confusion.
Descriptive statistics are correctly reported with mean and standard deviation; consider mentioning effect size measures to provide additional insight into practical significance.
Table 1 accurately displays means and SDs for each condition, facilitating clear comparison between rap and classical music recall.
The table caption repeats the title and lacks condition labels; refine the caption to clarify which statistics correspond to each music condition.
The bar chart correctly contrasts mean recall but axis labels and scaling are incomplete (y-axis not to 15); ensure full labeling and accurate title wording.
The choice of a Mann-Whitney U test is appropriate; adding a brief justification for using a non-parametric method would strengthen methodological transparency.
Findings are clearly linked to statistical results and the hypothesis, demonstrating confident interpretation of significance in relation to phonological overload.
The discussion of theory aligns with results, but consider addressing alternative explanations (e.g., primacy/recency effects) to deepen critical evaluation.
The integration with Perham & Sykoraâs work reinforces theoretical grounding, but the narrative is repetitive; streamline by focusing on novel insights from this study.
The evaluation of the independent-measures design correctly identifies internal validity strengths; expanding on potential external validity issues would enhance depth.
Linking matched-pairs modification to participant variability demonstrates a clear, justified resolution of the identified limitation.
The truncation of the modification description interrupts clarity; ensure all sentences are complete and that each modification is fully elaborated.
The proposed shift to random sampling is well justified and directly addresses concerns about generalisability, reflecting strong evaluation linkage.
The conclusion restates findings but could be strengthened by discussing practical implications and suggesting avenues for future research.
